target.name: Mainspring Energy
target.founder: Shannon Miller, Adam Simpson, Matt Svrcek

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mainspring Energy is a Series E climate and energy technology company that has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies in the Climate & Energy sector (including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape) and 1 public comparable (Lianzatech, ticker LNZA).

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields a valuation of $560M, applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $400M total raised proxy. The Peer-Median method cannot be reliably applied; while 21 same-sector peers exist, only 2 occupy adjacent stages and none form a sufficiently narrow cohort of 3+ comparables at matching stage and valuation tier. Drawing a median from all-sector peers would introduce unacceptable distortion.

The triangulated valuation range is $476M (low) to $644M (high), with a median of $560M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. Key risks include the reliance on stage-progression heuristics rather than peer multiples, the thinness of same-stage comps in deep-tech energy, and sensitivity to assumptions around typical step-up multier sizes across frontier-stage funding rounds. Additional data on comparable capital efficiency, unit economics, or path-to-revenue would materially sharpen this range.
